argument_persuasion_success.formal_description: P(view_change|x)=σ(b+Σ w_j z_j); an argument earns a delta. Executable. PREDICTIVE surface features on ONE platform; consistent with small persuasion effects.

citations.finding: meta-analytic means p̄≈0.03, q̄≈0.38; imitation >> innovation
citations.limits: SDs ≈ means (p SD≈0.03, q SD≈0.35) → transport as a WIDE prior, never a point; durable-goods/innovation only; not low-cost social behavior
citations.ref: Sultan, Farley & Lehmann 1990, J. Marketing Research 27(1):70-77
citations.study_population: 213 diffusion applications across 15 studies (durable goods, tech, services)

bass_diffusion.formal_description: dN/dt=(p+q·N/M)(M−N); p=external/innovation, q=internal/imitation; peak at t*=ln(q/p)/(p+q). Executable Euler rollout (bass_trajectory).
known_failure_modes: fails when exposure≠adoption (social behavior) or M is unknown
bass_diffusion.status_reason: verified published estimate / local fit valid in declared domain only
bass_diffusion.title: Bass finite-population innovation/imitation diffusion
bass_diffusion.uncertainty_note: p,q drawn from broad priors (meta-analytic SD ≈ mean); per-particle draw

citations.finding: randomized within-test traffic → CTR winner is causal for content response
citations.limits: clickbait-era Upworthy; identifies CONTENT response NOT position/examination bias; ~22% of tests had a caching-induced randomization failure (2024 correction) — content-response ordering signal is robust but caveated
citations.ref: Upworthy Research Archive (Matias, Munger, Le Quere, Ebersole 2021, Sci Data)

content_response_click.formal_description: surface features → fitted CTR layer → population-heterogeneity particle ranking (each audience particle clicks its argmax; variant score = win share). RANDOMIZED A/B traffic → the higher-CTR headline is CAUSALLY more clicked.
known_failure_modes: surface features alone (no population layer) ~random on P@1 — population is load-bearing (ablation preserved)
content_response_click.title: Content-response click ranking with audience heterogeneity
content_response_click.uncertainty_note: audience-heterogeneity particles (population plane); parameter widening on transport

exposure_response_hazard.formal_description: λ_i(t)=exp(θ·x), x=[1,log1p(k_i),log1p(deg_i),k_i/deg_i,exp(-recency/24)]; window activation P=1-exp(-∫λ dt), integrated event-by-event over the cohort subgraph with in-sample exposure growth (rollout).
known_failure_modes: rollout adds nothing when in-sample subgraph is sparse (honest scope)
exposure_response_hazard.title: Nonlinear log-linear exposure-response activation hazard
exposure_response_hazard.uncertainty_note: per-particle lognormal hazard-scale draw (parameter uncertainty); frailty sd fitted by profile likelihood
